curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 12 units, 18 chapters, 71 topics, 10 subtopics, and 77 learning outcomes extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Apply calorimetry techniques to measure enthalpy changes (∆U and ∆H)
Analyze the relationship between Gibbs Energy Change and chemical equilibrium
Evaluate the spontaneity of reactions based on thermodynamic parameters
Interpret reaction enthalpy in the context of different types of chemical reactions
Distinguish between physical and chemical processes in equilibrium
Calculate and apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Equilibrium Constant (K)
Analyze the relationship between Reaction Quotient (Q), Equilibrium Constant (K), and Gibbs Energy (G)
Predict factors affecting equilibria in homogeneous and heterogeneous systems
Apply ionic equilibrium concepts to acids, bases, buffer solutions, and solubility
Classify reactions using classical ideas of oxidation and reduction
Analyze redox reactions in terms of electron transfer mechanisms
Determine oxidation numbers for elements in compounds

marks_distribution: Class XI Chemistry Theory: Total Marks 70, Time 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XI Chemistry Practical: Total Marks 30
Formative Assessment Topics (No Summative Marks): s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State
question_paper_design: Theory Examination Structure based on Unit-wise weightage distribution
Practical Examination Structure based on Evaluation Scheme: Volumetric Analysis, Salt Analysis, Content Based Experiment, Project Work, Class Record and Viva
Special provisions for Visually Challenged Students with same evaluation scheme
competency_weightage: Atomic Models and Quantum Mechanical Model (Unit 2)
Chemical Bonding Theories (VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital) (Unit 4)
Thermodynamic Terms, Enthalpy Change, Spontaneity, Gibbs Energy (Unit 5)
Equilibrium in Physical and Chemical Processes, Ionic Equilibrium (Unit 6)
bloom_levels: Knowledge: Laws of Chemical Combination, Atomic Theory, Periodic Law
Understanding: Bohr's Model, VSEPR Theory, Bonding Theories
Application: Stoichiometric Calculations, Titration, pH Determination
Analysis: Electronic Configuration, Equilibrium Shifts, Qualitative Analysis
Evaluation: Project Work on Water Contamination, Acidity Testing
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment for s & p Block Elements (Unit 10 context)
Formative Assessment for The Gaseous State (Unit 11 context)
practical_assessment: Volumetric Analysis (8 Marks): Preparation of standard solutions, Titration of NaOH vs Oxalic Acid, HCl vs Sodium Carbonate
Salt Analysis (8 Marks): Determination of one anion and one cation in given salt
Content Based Experiment (6 Marks): Melting/Boiling point determination, Crystallization, pH studies, Chemical Equilibrium shifts
Basic Laboratory Techniques: Cutting glass tube, Bending, Drawing jet, Boring cork
project_assessment: Project Work (4 Marks): Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and information collection

Suggested Projects: Bacterial contamination in water, Water purification methods, Drinking water hardness analysis, Washing soap foaming capacity, Tea leaf acidity, Evaporation rate, Fiber tensile strength, Fruit/vegetable juice acidity
Requirement: Approximately 10 periods of work with teacher approval

marks_distribution: Class XI Chemistry Theory: Total Marks 70, Time 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XI Chemistry Practical: Total Marks 30
Formative Assessment Topics (No Summative Marks): s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State
question_paper_design: Theory Examination Structure based on Unit-wise weightage distribution
Practical Examination Structure based on Evaluation Scheme: Volumetric Analysis, Salt Analysis, Content Based Experiment, Project Work, Class Record and Viva
Special provisions for Visually Challenged Students with same evaluation scheme
competency_weightage: Atomic Models and Quantum Mechanical Model (Unit 2)
Chemical Bonding Theories (VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital) (Unit 4)
Thermodynamic Terms, Enthalpy Change, Spontaneity, Gibbs Energy (Unit 5)
Equilibrium in Physical and Chemical Processes, Ionic Equilibrium (Unit 6)
bloom_levels: Knowledge: Laws of Chemical Combination, Atomic Theory, Periodic Law
Understanding: Bohr's Model, VSEPR Theory, Bonding Theories
Application: Stoichiometric Calculations, Titration, pH Determination
Analysis: Electronic Configuration, Equilibrium Shifts, Qualitative Analysis
Evaluation: Project Work on Water Contamination, Acidity Testing
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment for s & p Block Elements (Unit 10 context)
Formative Assessment for The Gaseous State (Unit 11 context)
practical_assessment: Volumetric Analysis (8 Marks): Preparation of standard solutions, Titration of NaOH vs Oxalic Acid, HCl vs Sodium Carbonate
Salt Analysis (8 Marks): Determination of one anion and one cation in given salt
Content Based Experiment (6 Marks): Melting/Boiling point determination, Crystallization, pH studies, Chemical Equilibrium shifts
Basic Laboratory Techniques: Cutting glass tube, Bending, Drawing jet, Boring cork
project_assessment: Project Work (4 Marks): Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and information collection

Suggested Projects: Bacterial contamination in water, Water purification methods, Drinking water hardness analysis, Washing soap foaming capacity, Tea leaf acidity, Evaporation rate, Fiber tensile strength, Fruit/vegetable juice acidity
Requirement: Approximately 10 periods of work with teacher approval

Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XI Chemistry Practical: Total Marks 30
Formative Assessment Topics (No Summative Marks): s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State
question_paper_design: Theory Examination Structure based on Unit-wise weightage distribution
Practical Examination Structure based on Evaluation Scheme: Volumetric Analysis, Salt Analysis, Content Based Experiment, Project Work, Class Record and Viva
Special provisions for Visually Challenged Students with same evaluation scheme
competency_weightage: Atomic Models and Quantum Mechanical Model (Unit 2)
Chemical Bonding Theories (VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital) (Unit 4)
Thermodynamic Terms, Enthalpy Change, Spontaneity, Gibbs Energy (Unit 5)
Equilibrium in Physical and Chemical Processes, Ionic Equilibrium (Unit 6)
bloom_levels: Knowledge: Laws of Chemical Combination, Atomic Theory, Periodic Law
Understanding: Bohr's Model, VSEPR Theory, Bonding Theories
Application: Stoichiometric Calculations, Titration, pH Determination
Analysis: Electronic Configuration, Equilibrium Shifts, Qualitative Analysis
Evaluation: Project Work on Water Contamination, Acidity Testing
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment for s & p Block Elements (Unit 10 context)
Formative Assessment for The Gaseous State (Unit 11 context)
practical_assessment: Volumetric Analysis (8 Marks): Preparation of standard solutions, Titration of NaOH vs Oxalic Acid, HCl vs Sodium Carbonate
Salt Analysis (8 Marks): Determination of one anion and one cation in given salt
Content Based Experiment (6 Marks): Melting/Boiling point determination, Crystallization, pH studies, Chemical Equilibrium shifts
Basic Laboratory Techniques: Cutting glass tube, Bending, Drawing jet, Boring cork
project_assessment: Project Work (4 Marks): Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and information collection

Suggested Projects: Bacterial contamination in water, Water purification methods, Drinking water hardness analysis, Washing soap foaming capacity, Tea leaf acidity, Evaporation rate, Fiber tensile strength, Fruit/vegetable juice acidity
Requirement: Approximately 10 periods of work with teacher approval
